Most students look at their score, feel bad, and move on. This workflow turns every mock into 3× the learning value. Time required: 2-3 hours after each mock When: Within 24 hours of the test Why This Workflow Matters Your mock test score tells you what happened. AI analysis tells you why and what to do next . The 4-Phase Mock Review Phase 1: Categorize Every Wrong Answer (30 min) First, go through every question you got wrong. Categorize each: Type A: "I didn't know the concept" — knowledge gap Type B: "I knew it but made a silly mistake" — carelessness Type C: "I was confused between 2 options" — weak understanding Type D: "I didn't have time" — time management Type E: "I guessed wrong" — acceptable Then run this prompt: I just completed a [JEE Main/NEET/CBSE] mock test. My results: - Total score: [X/Y] - Physics: [score] | Mistakes: [list topic of each wrong Q] - Chemistry: [score] | Mistakes: [list topic of each wrong Q] - Maths/Biology: [score] | Mistakes: [list topic of each wrong Q] Error breakdown: - Knowledge gaps (Type A): [topics] - Silly mistakes (Type B): [count] - Conceptual confusion (Type C): [topics] - Time issues (Type D): [sections] Please: 1. Identify my top 3 weak areas by category 2. Tell me if any pattern exists (e.g., "You keep making errors in electrostatics application") 3. Prioritize what to fix first for maximum score improvement 4. Build a 3-day recovery plan Phase 2: Deep-Dive on Type A Errors (60 min) For every knowledge gap topic: I got questions on [TOPIC] wrong in my mock test because I didn't know the concept. The question(s) I got wrong: [PASTE QUESTION IF YOU HAVE IT] Please: 1. Explain the concept from the beginning 2. Show me how to solve this type of question 3. Give me 3 similar questions with solutions 4. Tell me what to memorize to never get this wrong again Phase 3: Fix Type B (Silly Mistakes) (20 min) I made [X] silly mistakes in today's mock despite knowing the concepts. My mistakes were: [describe 2-3 examples] Help me: 1. Identify if there's a pattern to my silly mistakes 2. Give me a "before submitting" mental checklist 3. Suggest 2-3 habits to reduce careless errors 4. Tell me how top JEE/NEET scorers approach this Phase 4: Build the Recovery Plan (20 min) Based on my mock test analysis: - Weak areas: [from Phase 1] - Days until next mock/exam: [X] Create a day-by-day study plan: - What to study each day - Which topics to prioritize vs maintain - How many practice questions per topic - When to take the next mock Format: Day 1, Day 2, Day 3... table format. 📋 Mock Review Checklist [ ] Categorized every wrong answer (A/B/C/D/E) [ ] Ran Phase 1 analysis prompt [ ] Fixed all Type A knowledge gaps [ ] Addressed silly mistake patterns [ ] Created recovery plan [ ] Scheduled next mock test 📈 Track Your Progress Keep a mock test log: Date Mock Score Top 3 Weak Areas Fixed? [date] Mock 1 [score] [topics] [ ] After 5 mocks, run: Here are my last 5 mock test scores and weak areas: [paste your log] Show me: am I improving? What's my persistent weakness? text: 📖 New Chapter Workflow | AI Atlas Skip to content AI Atlas Menu Home Study Systems Prompt Library Workflows Tools Database Resources Career Guides About Search Contributing Roadmap GitHub Home / Workflows / 📖 New Chapter Workflow workflows 📖 New Chapter Workflow How to study any new chapter using AI — from first read to exam-ready. Time required: 2-4 hours Works for: JEE, NEET, CBSE any class Best AI tool: Claude (fre Explore Systems Browse Resources 📖 New Chapter Workflow How to study any new chapter using AI — from first read to exam-ready. Time required: 2-4 hours Works for: JEE, NEET, CBSE any class Best AI tool: Claude (free tier works) The 5-Step Chapter Workflow ✅ Step 1: Reconnaissance (10 min) Before reading a single page, run this prompt: I'm about to start [CHAPTER NAME] in [SUBJECT] for [EXAM]. I haven't studied it yet. Tell me: 1. What is this chapter fundamentally about? (3 sentences) 2. What should I already know before starting? 3. What are the 3 most important concepts in this chapter? 4. How is this chapter tested in [EXAM]? (question types, weightage) 5. What's the #1 mistake students make with this chapter? Why: Knowing where you're going makes reading 3× faster. ✅ Step 2: Concept-by-Concept Study (60-90 min) Read one section of your textbook/notes. Then for each concept: I just read about [CONCEPT] in [CHAPTER]. Here's what I think I understood: [YOUR EXPLANATION IN 2-3 SENTENCES] Check my understanding: 1. Am I right? What did I miss or misunderstand? 2. Clarify the part most students get wrong 3. Give me one worked example with full solution 4. One question to test if I've really got it Loop this for every major concept in the chapter. ✅ Step 3: Connect the Chapter (20 min) After covering all concepts: I've now studied all of [CHAPTER]. Help me see the full picture: 1. How do all the concepts in this chapter relate to each other? 2. What's the logical flow from start to finish? 3. Which concepts depend on which? 4. How does this chapter connect to [PREVIOUS CHAPTERS]? 5. Create a 10-point summary of the entire chapter. ✅ Step 4: Exam-Focused Preparation (30 min) I've finished studying [CHAPTER] for [EXAM]. Now help me prepare specifically for the exam: 1. List the 5 most likely question types from this chapter 2. Show me 2 examples of each type (with solutions) 3. What shortcuts or tricks apply? 4. Create a "cheat sheet" — what I'd write on a flashcard 5. Common traps: what wrong answers look like and why they're wrong ✅ Step 5: Self-Test (20-30 min) The most important step. Don't skip it. Quiz me on [CHAPTER] with 12 questions: - 4 easy (recall/definition level) - 5 medium (application) - 3 hard ([EXAM]-level) Ask one question at a time. Wait for my answer before showing the next. After each answer: confirm right/wrong, brief explanation. At the end: tell me my score and which concepts to re-study. 📋 Chapter Study Checklist [ ] Step 1: Got the overview before reading [ ] Step 2: Checked understanding after each concept [ ] Step 3: Connected all concepts to each other [ ] Step 4: Did exam-focused prep [ ] Step 5: Scored 7+/12 on self-test [ ] Added chapter to revision schedule ⏰ Compressed Version (When Short on Time) If you only have 45 minutes: Step 1 (5 min) — reconnaissance prompt Step 2 condensed — one prompt covering all concepts at once Step 5 — 8-question quiz ← Back to Workflows · Suggest improvement Previous 📊 Mock Test Review Workflow Next JEE AI Prompt Library Related Resources Continue Exploring workflows 📊 Mock Test Review Workflow Turn every mock test into a precise improvement plan. Claude is built by Anthropic with a focus on thoughtful, accu Explore Systems Browse Resources 🤖 Claude for Students — Complete Guide Claude is the best AI for deep study. Here's exactly how to use it. Why Claude for Studying? Claude is built by Anthropic with a focus on thoughtful, accurate explanations. For students: Deepest explanations — explains concepts, not just answers Long context — can hold your entire study session in memory Honest — tells you when it's uncertain Great at maths — step-by-step working shown clearly Essay feedback — nuanced writing quality feedback 🆓 Getting Started (Free) Go to claude.ai Create a free account (email) Free tier: Claude Sonnet — excellent for studying No credit card needed Free tier limits: ~20-30 messages before a daily limit. Resets each day. 💡 How to Get the Best Results Tip 1: Tell Claude your context I'm a Class 12 student preparing for JEE Main. I have a strong understanding of Class 11 physics but Class 12 topics are new to me. Explain at that level. Tip 2: Ask for what you need specifically ❌ "Explain Newton's Laws" ✅ "Explain Newton's Third Law specifically for problems involving two blocks connected by a string — this is where I get confused in JEE problems" Tip 3: Push for clarity If an explanation isn't clear: "Explain point 2 again, but simpler" "I still don't understand [X]. Try a different analogy" "Show me a worked example for this" Tip 4: Use it as a conversation Don't start fresh each session for the same topic. Build on previous messages: "Now apply that concept to this problem..." "What's the exception to the rule you just explained?" "Connect this to what you told me about [topic] earlier" 📚 Best Use Cases for Claude Use Case How Well Prompt Template Concept explanation ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Explain [X] for JEE level" Step-by-step problem solving ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Solve this step by step, explain each step" Essay writing / feedback ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Review my essay and suggest improvements" Generating practice questions ⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Give me 5 [level] questions on [topic]" Summarizing long content ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Summarize this chapter's key points" Maths derivations ⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Derive [formula] with each step explained" ⚠️ When NOT to Use Claude Real-time search — Claude's knowledge has a cutoff date. Use Perplexity for current info. Solving photos of questions — Use ChatGPT or Gemini (better image processing) Hindi-medium content — Claude works in Hindi but ChatGPT/Gemini are better When you need source citations — Use Perplexity 🔥 Power User Techniques Create a Study Persona Paste this at the start of a study session: For this conversation, you are my personal JEE tutor. You know my level: [describe your level]. Your teaching style should be: [Socratic/direct/ example-heavy]. When I make mistakes, point them out directly. Never give me the answer without making me think first. The Feynman Test I'm going to explain [CONCEPT] to you as if you know nothing. After I finish, tell me: 1. What did I explain correctly? 2. Where was I wrong or incomplete? 3. What important points did I miss? Ready? Asset Organisation System 2.1 Master Folder Structure Every file in the AI Atlas repository lives in exactly one of the following directories. No exceptions. ai-atlas/ │ ├── meta/ # Platform documentation (not student-facing) │ ├── ai-atlas-operating-system.md │ ├── content-standards.md │ ├── project-vision.md │ └── changelog.md │ ├── sitemap/ # Platform architecture │ └── ai-atlas-sitemap.md │ ├── templates/ # Reusable content templates │ ├── Universal-Chapter-Template.md │ ├── Prompt-Template.md │ ├── Workflow-Template.md │ └── Tool-Profile-Template.md │ ├── prompts/ # All prompt libraries │ ├── jee/ │ │ ├── JEE-AI-Prompt-Library.md # 50 JEE prompts (complete) │ │ ├── jee-physics-prompts.md # Subject-specific extraction (future) │ │ ├── jee-chemistry-prompts.md │ │ └── jee-mathematics-prompts.md │ ├── neet/ │ │ └── NEET-AI-Prompt-Library.md # (missing — see Section 14) │ └── school/ │ └── School-AI-Prompt-Library.md # (missing — see Section 14) │ ├── workflows/ # All study and operational workflows │ ├── AI-Educational-Workflows.md # 25 workflows (complete) │ └── subject-specific/ # Future subject extractions │ ├── jee-physics-workflow.md │ ├── neet-biology-workflow.md │ └── class10-science-workflow.md │ ├── tools/ # AI tools database │ ├── AI-Tools-Database.md # 100 tools (complete) │ └── tool-profiles/ # Future deep-dive tool pages │ ├── claude-for-students.md │ ├── anki-setup-guide.md │ └── notion-study-system.md │ ├── study-systems/ # Complete exam study systems │ ├── jee/ │ │ ├── jee-main-study-system.md │ │ ├── jee-advanced-study-system.md │ │ ├── jee-physics-system.md # (missing — see Section 14) │ │ ├── jee-chemistry-system.md # (missing) │ │ └── jee-mathematics-system.md # (missing) │ ├── neet/ │ │ ├── neet-complete-study-system.md # (missing) │ │ ├── neet-biology-system.md # (missing) │ │ └── neet-physics-chemistry.md # (missing) │ └── school/ │ ├── class10-board-system.md # (missing) │ ├── class12-board-system.md # (missing) │ └── class6-8-foundation.md # (missing) │ ├── chapters/ # Individual chapter pages (future — highest volume) │ ├── jee-physics/ │ │ ├── mechanics/ │ │ ├── thermodynamics/ │ │ ├── electromagnetism/ │ │ ├── optics/ │ │ └── modern-physics/ │ ├── jee-chemistry/ │ │ ├── physical/ │ │ ├── organic/ │ │ └── inorganic/ │ ├── jee-mathematics/ │ │ ├── algebra/ │ │ ├── calculus/ │ │ ├── coordinate-geometry/ │ │ └── trigonometry/ │ ├── neet-biology/ │ │ ├── botany/ │ │ └── zoology/ │ └── school-science/ │ ├── class9/ │ └── class10/ │ ├── career-guides/ # Career and after-exam content (future) │ ├── after-jee.md │ ├── after-neet.md │ └── ai-careers.md │ ├── resources/ # Curated external resources (future) │ ├── free-books.md │ ├── youtube-channels.md │ └── practice-sites.md │ └── assets/ # Images, diagrams, and static files ├── images/ ├── diagrams/ └── pdfs/ 2.2 Directory Rules Rule 1 — One file, one home. Every content file has exactly one canonical location. No duplicates. Symlinks are permitted in the navigation index only. Rule 2 — Subject directories first. When a file belongs to a subject (JEE Physics, NEET Biology), it always goes inside the subject subdirectory, not at the parent level. Rule 3 — Meta never mixes with content. The meta/ directory is internal infrastructure. Nothing inside meta/ is linked from student-facing pages. Rule 4 — Templates stay in templates/ . No template file lives inside content directories. Content files reference templates; they do not contain them. Rule 5 — Assets travel with content. Any image or diagram created for a specific chapter goes in assets/images/[chapter-slug]/ . 3. Repository Navigation System 3.1 Navigation Architecture AI Atlas uses a three-layer navigation system: Layer 1 — Repository README.md (entry point, top-level navigation) │ ├── Layer 2 — Category INDEX.md files (one per major directory) │ (sitemap/INDEX.md, prompts/INDEX.md, workflows/INDEX.md, etc.) │ └── Layer 3 — Individual content files (the actual pages) 3.2 README.md Structure (Root) The root README.md is the only file a new visitor needs to find everything. It must always contain: # AI Atlas [One sentence: what it is] [One sentence: who it's for] [One sentence: it's free] ## Quick Start For JEE students → [link] For NEET students → [link] For school students → [link] New to AI? → [link] ## What's Here | Section | What's Inside | Files | |---------|--------------|-------| | Prompts | ... | ... | | Workflows | ... | ... | ... ## Recently Added [last 5 additions with dates] ## Contributing [link to contribution guide] The README must be updated every time a file is added or modifi

title: Anki Setup Guide for JEE/NEET
description: AI Atlas → AI Tools → Anki Setup Guide Anki is the most scientifically proven tool for long-term retention. This guide gets you set up in 30 minutes. --- Why
text: Anki Setup Guide for JEE/NEET | AI Atlas Skip to content AI Atlas Menu Home Study Systems Prompt Library Workflows Tools Database Resources Career Guides About Search Contributing Roadmap GitHub Home / AI Tools / Tool Profiles / Anki Setup Guide for JEE/NEET tools Anki Setup Guide for JEE/NEET AI Atlas → AI Tools → Anki Setup Guide Anki is the most scientifically proven tool for long-term retention. This guide gets you set up in 30 minutes. --- Why Explore Systems Browse Resources Anki Setup Guide for JEE/NEET AI Atlas → AI Tools → Anki Setup Guide Anki is the most scientifically proven tool for long-term retention. This guide gets you set up in 30 minutes. Why Anki for JEE/NEET? Anki uses spaced repetition — it shows you cards just before you're about to forget them. This means: Formulas stick permanently, not just for the exam Biology terms actually stay in memory (critical for NEET) Review time stays manageable even as your deck grows Mobile sync — study on phone between classes The math: 15 minutes of Anki daily > 2 hours of re-reading the same notes. Install Anki (Free, all platforms) Platform Download Cost Windows/Mac/Linux apps.ankiweb.net Free iPhone (AnkiMobile) App Store ₹750 one-time Android (AnkiDroid) Play Store Free Web (AnkiWeb) ankiweb.net Free Best setup: Desktop app for adding cards + AnkiDroid (Android) for reviewing on phone. Sync is free. First Deck Setup — 30 Minutes Step 1: Create Your Account Go to ankiweb.net Create a free account Open the desktop app, sign in → syncs across devices Step 2: Create Your First Deck Structure My JEE Decks/ ├── Physics/ │ ├── Mechanics │ ├── Electromagnetism │ └── Modern Physics ├── Chemistry/ │ ├── Physical │ ├── Organic │ └── Inorganic └── Mathematics/ ├── Calculus └── Algebra Create nested decks: Decks → Create Deck → Name: "JEE::Physics::Mechanics" Step 3: Optimal Card Types for JEE/NEET Type 1: Formula Cards Front: Formula name + "What is it?" Back: Formula + when to use it + units Type 2: Concept Cards Front: "What happens when [condition]?" Back: The answer + why + example Type 3: Cloze Deletion (best for NEET Biology) Example: "The process of {{c1::photosynthesis}} produces {{c2::glucose}} and {{c3::oxygen}}" Anki Settings Optimised for JEE/NEET Go to Deck → Options and set: Setting Value Why New cards per day 20–30 Sustainable rate Maximum reviews/day 200 Don't let backlog build Starting ease 250% Standard Easy interval 4 days First easy step Graduating interval 1 day Forces repetition early Create Cards With AI The fastest way to build your Anki deck: Open Claude and paste: "Create 20 Anki flashcards for [CHAPTER] for JEE. Format each card as: FRONT: [question or concept] BACK: [answer, formula, or explanation] Focus on: formulas, definitions, common misconceptions, and application questions. Include 5 cloze-deletion format cards for important definitions." Copy the output → paste into Anki. Done. Daily Review Routine Morning (10 min): Review due cards on AnkiDroid on phone → While commuting / eating breakfast Evening (15 min): Add 10–20 new cards for today's study topic → After finishing a chapter Night (5 min): Quick review on phone before sleep → Reinforces memory during sleep Import Cards from CSV (Bulk Import) Create a spreadsheet: Column A = front, Column B = back Export as CSV Anki → File → Import → select CSV Map columns to Front/Back fields Generate the CSV with AI: "Create a CSV-formatted Anki deck for [CHAPTER]. Format: front_text,back_text No header row. 30 cards. Focus on high-priority concepts for JEE." Most JEE/NEET failures originate from weak Class 11 foundations. Class 11 = 50% of JEE/NEET syllabus. Don't rush it. The Class 11 Trap Many students breeze through Class 11, score well in school tests (which are easy), and crash in Class 12 when the foundations aren't there. AI helps you avoid this: "I'm a Class 11 student who just studied [TOPIC]. Test my conceptual understanding — don't ask me to recite definitions. Ask me questions that reveal whether I truly understand or just memorised. Be tough." Subject Focus Physics: Mechanics is the most critical. Every other topic builds on it. Chemistry: Physical Chemistry foundations (Mole Concept, Thermodynamics) are essential. Mathematics: Calculus starts here. Integration and Differentiation are lifelong tools. Then give me 3 practice problems at increasing difficulty with solutions." 20 Ready-to-Use Claude Prompts Learning New Concepts Prompt 1 — First Principles Explainer "Explain [TOPIC] for JEE from absolute first principles. Assume I know basic [PREREQUISITE] but nothing else. Use analogies I can visualise. Then show me how this concept appears in JEE questions." Prompt 2 — Concept Builder "I'm studying [CHAPTER] for JEE/NEET. I understand the basic definition but I don't understand WHY it works. Explain the underlying reasoning, then show me a common misconception students have and why it's wrong." Prompt 3 — Visual Explanation Request "Explain [TOPIC] as if you're drawing a diagram. Describe each element, what it represents, and how the parts connect. I'll draw it as you describe." Doubt Solving Prompt 4 — Specific Doubt "In [CHAPTER], I don't understand why [SPECIFIC THING]. I thought [MY WRONG REASONING]. Where is my thinking wrong? Correct my understanding without just giving me the answer." Prompt 5 — Derivation Breakdown "Break down the derivation of [FORMULA/RESULT] for me. Do it step by step. At each step, explain WHY that mathematical operation is valid and what it means physically." Revision Prompt 6 — Quick Formula Sheet "Create a complete formula sheet for [CHAPTER] for JEE. Include: formula, variables definition, when to use it, common mistakes. Format as a clean table." Prompt 7 — 10-Point Revision Checklist "Create a 10-point revision checklist for [CHAPTER]. Each point should be something I can actively verify I understand, not just 'read the chapter'." Prompt 8 — Rapid Fire Questions "Give me 15 rapid-fire conceptual questions on [CHAPTER]. After I answer each one (say 'next'), tell me if I'm right and correct any misunderstanding briefly." PYQ Analysis Prompt 9 — PYQ Pattern Analysis "Based on JEE Main PYQs, what are the 5 most commonly tested concepts from [CHAPTER]? For each concept, give me one example question type and what the examiner is testing." Prompt 10 — Difficulty Level Training "Create 3 questions on [TOPIC]: 1. JEE Main level (straightforward application) 2. JEE Advanced level (multi-concept) 3. JEE Advanced level (tricky/non-standard) Solve all three with full working." Mock Analysis Prompt 11 — Mock Error Analysis "I got [TOPIC/QUESTION TYPE] wrong in my mock test. My approach was: [YOUR APPROACH] The correct answer was: [CORRECT ANSWER] Diagnose exactly where my reasoning failed and what I need to revise to avoid this mistake." Prompt 12 — Weakness Identification "I consistently struggle with [TOPIC AREA]. Ask me 5 diagnostic questions to identify exactly which sub-concept I'm weak in. Then create a targeted 2-hour study plan to fix it." Advanced Tips Upload your mock answer sheet: [Upload: photo of your mock test answers] "Analyse my answers. Identify my error patterns. What topics do I need to focus on most?" Use Claude as a Socratic tutor: "Don't give me the answer directly. Ask me guiding questions that help me figure out [CONCEPT] myself." Create personalised notes: "Based on our conversation, create structured notes on [TOPIC] that I can save and revise. Include: key concepts, formulas, common pitfalls, and 3 example questions." JEE Advanced vs JEE Main — Key Differences Aspect JEE Main JEE Advanced Question type Mostly single-concept Multi-concept, multi-step Integer type Some Significant weightage Multi-correct No Yes — partial marking Difficulty Moderate High — often novel Strategy Accuracy + speed Deep understanding The AI difference: For JEE Advanced, use AI to build understanding , not just solve problems. The exam tests whether you truly understand — you can't template your way through it. Advanced AI Workflow for JEE Advanced Chapters For each chapter, run this sequence: 1. UNDERSTAND: "Explain [topic] from absolute first principles. I want to know WHY every formula is derived, not just HOW." 2. CONNECT: "What are all the JEE Advanced topics that connect to [topic]? Show me multi-concept problems that use this." 3. PREDICT: "What are the non-standard question types from [topic] that have appeared in JEE Advanced? What makes them tricky?" 4. VERIFY: "Ask me 5 questions that distinguish someone who truly understands [topic] vs someone who only memorised formulas." High-Priority Topics for JEE Advanced Physics Rotational Mechanics (highest weightage) Electrostatics + Capacitors Optics (especially Wave Optics) Modern Physics — Photoelectric, Nuclear Thermodynamics Chemistry Organic Chemistry — all mechanisms Coordination Compounds Electrochemistry P-Block elements (Groups 15, 16, 17) Mathematics Integration (single highest marks chapter) Complex Numbers P&C + Probability 3D Geometry + Vectors Integer Type Practice with AI "Generate 10 integer-type questions on [TOPIC] at JEE Advanced level. For integer type: the answer is a single integer 0–9. Questions should require multi-step reasoning. Provide complete solutions after I attempt them." Multi-Correct Practice "Generate 5 multi-correct MCQs on [TOPIC]. Format: 4 options, possibly more than one correct. At JEE Advanced difficulty — include options that look correct but have subtle errors." title: JEE AI Prompt Library
description: 50 High-Quality Prompts for AI-Powered JEE Preparation Platform: AI Atlas Subject: JEE Main + Advanced (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics) Tools: Claude, ChatGP
text: JEE AI Prompt Library | AI Atlas Skip to content AI Atlas Menu Home Study Systems Prompt Library Workflows Tools Database Resources Career Guides About Search Contributing Roadmap GitHub Home / JEE AI Prompt Library Core JEE AI Prompt Library 50 High-Quality Prompts for AI-Powered JEE Preparation Platform: AI Atlas Subject: JEE Main + Advanced (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics) Tools: Claude, ChatGP Explore Systems Browse Resources JEE AI Prompt Library 50 High-Quality Prompts for AI-Powered JEE Preparation Platform: AI Atlas Subject: JEE Main + Advanced (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics) Tools: Claude, ChatGPT (free), Gemini (free) Total Prompts: 50 across 5 categories How to use this library Copy any prompt, replace the bracketed placeholders with your actual content, and paste into Claude or any free AI tool. Each prompt is designed to replace 30–60 minutes of conventional studying. Category 1 — Learning (10 Prompts) Use these when encountering a topic for the first time. Goal: build deep conceptual understanding before solving a single problem. L-01 — First-Principles Concept Builder Purpose Build intuition for any JEE topic from the ground up, starting with the physical or mathematical idea behind the formula rather than the formula itself. Prompt Teach me [TOPIC] for JEE [Main/Advanced] from first principles. Structure your response as: 1. The core physical/mathematical intuition — what is actually happening? 2. How that intuition leads to the key equation (derive it step by step) 3. What each variable in the equation represents physically 4. The conditions under which the formula holds — and when it breaks 5. The top 3 conceptual traps JEE uses from this topic Do not start with the formula. Start with the idea. Example Input Teach me Rotational Kinetic Energy for JEE Advanced from first principles. Optimization Tips Add "Use an analogy from everyday life before introducing any equation" for stronger retention. If the output feels too textbook, add: "Explain as if I already understand linear motion perfectly but have never heard of rotation." Run this prompt before watching any video on the topic — it gives your brain a framework to hang the video's content onto. L-02 — Technique Identification Trainer Purpose Train the most valuable JEE skill: recognising which technique or method a question is asking for within the first 10 seconds of reading it. Prompt For the topic [TOPIC] in JEE, teach me how to identify which technique to use before I start solving. For each major technique or method in this topic: 1. What signals in the question point to this technique? (exact language, given quantities, what's asked) 2. What is the very first line I should write when I recognise this technique? 3. What does the wrong technique look like being applied — and how do I catch myself? 4. Give one example question and show how the signals led you to the right technique. Format as a technique identification guide, not a solution manual. Example Input For Integration in JEE, teach me how to identify which technique to use before I start solving. Optimization Tips Ask this after you've learned the basics of a topic. It works best when you already know the techniques but confuse which to apply. Add: "Include questions where two techniques seem equally valid — and explain the tiebreaker." Use the output as a laminated quick-reference card for that chapter. L-03 — Feynman Gap-Finder Purpose Force deep understanding by explaining a concept back to the AI, which then identifies exactly where your mental model has holes. Prompt I'm going to explain [TOPIC] to you as if you're a class 9 student who has never heard of it. After I finish, please: 1. List every statement I made that is incorrect or imprecise 2. List every important concept I missed entirely 3. List every place I oversimplified in a way that would cause errors in JEE problems 4. Rate my overall understanding out of 10 and explain the rating Here is my explanation: [YOUR EXPLANATION IN YOUR OWN WORDS] Example Input I'm going to explain Electric Flux and Gauss's Law to you as if you're a class 9 student... [Student writes: "Electric flux is the number of electric field lines passing through a surface. Gauss's Law says if you draw any closed surface, the total flux through it equals the charge enclosed divided by epsilon-naught. You can use it when there's symmetry to find E without integration."] Optimization Tips The worse your explanation, the more valuable the feedback. Don't try to sound smart — try to expose your gaps. After getting the feedback, redo the explanation incorporating corrections. Repeat until you score 9/10. Use this 24 hours after initially learning a topic, not immediately. L-04 — Geometric Intuition Builder (Mathematics) Purpose Most JEE Mathematics problems have a hidden geometric picture. This prompt teaches you to see algebra geometrically, which is how top scorers solve problems 3x faster. Prompt For the algebraic result / formula [FORMULA OR THEOREM], give me: 1. The geometric interpretation — what does this look like when drawn? 2. A physical situation where this result appears naturally 3. How visualising the geometry makes the algebra obvious 4. Three JEE problems where seeing the geometry reduces a 10-step algebra solution to 2–3 steps 5. What most students miss by never thinking about the geometry Topic: [TOPIC / CHAPTER] Example Input For the Cauchy-Schwarz Inequality, give me the geometric interpretation... Topic: Algebra / Inequalities (JEE Advanced) Optimization Tips Use this prompt specifically for topics that feel purely algebraic: sequences and series, complex numbers, P&C. These all have deep geometric interpretations. Ask follow-up: "Draw this in a coordinate plane and describe what I'd see." Pair with Desmos (free) to actually plot what Claude describes. L-05 — Reaction Mechanism Narrator (Chemistry — Organic) Purpose Organic Chemistry clicks when reactions are understood as stories of electron movement, not memorised as lists. This prompt builds mechanism intuition. Prompt Explain the [REACTION NAME] mechanism as a step-by-step narrative for JEE. For each step: 1. Which electrons move and where do they go? (describe the arrow-pushing in words) 2. Why do they move in that direction? (electronegativity, stability, resonance — explain the driving force) 3. What intermediate is formed and how stable is it? 4. What would happen if conditions changed? (solvent polarity, nucleophile strength, etc.) After the mechanism: - What is the rate-determining step and why? - How does JEE test this mechanism? (question types, common traps) - What is the one-line memory hook for this mechanism? Example Input Explain the SN2 mechanism as a step-by-step narrative for JEE. Optimization Tips After getting the narrative, close the chat and redraw the mechanism from memory. Add: "Now contrast this mechanism with [related mechanism] — show exactly where the electron paths diverge." Use this prompt for every named reaction in Organic Chemistry. ~40 reactions × 20 minutes = 13 hours of deep organic understanding. L-06 — NCERT Testable Fact Extractor (NEET / Inorganic) Purpose NCERT contains hundreds of sentences. Not all are equally JEE-testable. This prompt extracts only the high-value content so you don't waste time on low-yield lines. Prompt Analyse [NCERT CHAPTER NAME, CLASS 11/12] for JEE [Main/Advanced]. For every paragraph or section, classify each fact as: - MUST KNOW: Directly tested in JEE multiple times - SHOULD KNOW: Tested occasionally or as part of a larger question - LOW PRIORITY: Rarely or never directly tested For MUST KNOW facts, also give: - The exact JEE question format this fact appears in - The most common wrong answer students choose and why Output as a prioritised study guide, not a summary. Example Input Analyse NCERT Class 12 Chapter 9 — Coordination Compounds for JEE Main. Optimization Tips Run this before reading NCERT, not after — it tells you what to pay attention

Train yourself to identify the right technique in the first 30 seconds of reading a problem. Technique-First AI Approach "I'm studying [CHAPTER] for JEE. Don't give me problems yet. Dedicate 3–4x more time to it. "I'm studying Integration for JEE. Create a structured guide to all integration techniques in order of: 1. Frequency in JEE 2. Difficulty For each technique: definition, when to use, 2 worked examples." Speed Training with AI "Give me 20 single-step Maths problems from [CHAPTER]. I should be able to solve each in under 90 seconds. After I finish (tell me when), analyse which types took longest." text: NEET — Complete AI Prompt Library | AI Atlas Skip to content AI Atlas Menu Home Study Systems Prompt Library Workflows Tools Database Resources Career Guides About Search Contributing Roadmap GitHub Home / Prompt Libraries / NEET / NEET — Complete AI Prompt Library prompts NEET — Complete AI Prompt Library AI Atlas → Prompts → NEET 50 AI prompts for NEET UG aspirants. NCERT-first approach. Biology-heavy. --- Category Overview Category Prompts Focus ------------- Explore Systems Browse Resources NEET — Complete AI Prompt Library AI Atlas → Prompts → NEET 50 AI prompts for NEET UG aspirants. NCERT-first approach. Biology-heavy. Category Overview Category Prompts Focus NCERT Mastery (N) N-01 to N-10 Deep NCERT understanding Diagram Mastery (D) D-01 to D-10 Visual learning for Biology Assertion-Reason (A) A-01 to A-10 NEET-specific question type PYQ Analysis (P) P-01 to P-10 Past paper strategy Mock Analysis (M) M-01 to M-10 Post-test improvement NCERT Mastery Prompts (N-01 to N-10) N-01 — Reading Guide "Before I read NCERT Biology [Chapter Name] Class [11/12], create a reading guide with: - 15 key concepts to look for - 5 questions to answer while reading - Terms I must define precisely - Which diagrams to focus on" N-02 — NCERT Comprehension Check "Quiz me on NCERT Biology [Chapter] Class [11/12]. Ask 20 direct questions from the NCERT text. After I answer, tell me if my answer matches NCERT exactly. Flag any additions or omissions." N-03 — Keyword Extraction "Extract all bold and important terms from NCERT Biology [Chapter] Class [11/12]. For each term: - NCERT definition - Why it's important for NEET - Has it been asked in NEET PYQs? (if you know)" N-04 — Exception and Special Cases "List all exceptions, anomalies and special cases in NCERT Biology [Chapter]. NEET loves to test these. Format: Exception → Rule it breaks → Why it's different" N-05 — One-Line Summary Cards "Summarise every paragraph of NCERT Biology [Chapter] in exactly one sentence. These will be my rapid revision cards." N-06 — Comparison Tables "From NCERT Biology [Chapter], create all possible comparison tables (e.g., Mitosis vs Meiosis). These are high-yield for NEET MCQs." N-07 — Fill in the Blanks "Convert the key paragraphs of NCERT Biology [Chapter] into fill-in-the-blank exercises. The blanks should be important terms, numbers, or processes. Include answer key." N-08 — Process Narration "Narrate [biological process] as a story with a beginning, middle and end. Include all steps NCERT mentions. Make it vivid enough that I can visualise it happening." N-09 — NCERT vs Exam Format "Take the key content from NCERT Biology [Chapter] and convert it into NEET MCQ format. 10 questions, 4 options each, answer key with explanation." N-10 — Revision in 5 Minutes "Give me a 5-minute revision of NCERT Biology [Chapter]. Most important 10 facts only. Bullet points. Include any numbers or statistics NCERT mentions." Diagram Mastery Prompts (D-01 to D-10) D-01 — Diagram Description "Describe the diagram of [biological structure/process] for NEET in complete detail. Tell me: what it looks like, all labelled parts, the function of each part, what questions NEET asks about it." D-02 — Label Drill "I've drawn [diagram name]. Give me a label quiz: Point to 10 parts by describing their position. I'll name the part. You correct me." D-03 — Process Diagrams "Walk me through the diagram of [process — e.g. mitosis stages] stage by stage. For each stage: what is happening, what it looks like, which NEET questions ask about this specific stage." Assertion-Reason Prompts (A-01 to A-10) A-01 — Generate A-R Questions "Generate 15 assertion-reason questions on [Chapter] for NEET. Use the standard NEET format: (a) Both A and R true, R explains A (b) Both true, R doesn't explain A (c) A true, R false (d) A false, R true Mix all four types. Include detailed explanations." A-02 — Difficult A-R "Generate 5 tricky assertion-reason questions on [Chapter] where the assertion is true but the reason is subtly wrong. Explain exactly where students get confused." PYQ Analysis Prompts (P-01 to P-10) P-01 — Chapter PYQ Summary "Based on NEET PYQs, what are the top 10 concepts from [Chapter] that are tested most frequently? For each: number of times asked, typical question format, what the question tests." P-02 — PYQ Difficulty Analysis "Analyse NEET questions from [Chapter] by difficulty. Which concepts appear in difficult questions that most students get wrong? What's the conceptual gap?" Mock Analysis Prompts (M-01 to M-10) M-01 — Biology Error Analysis "In my NEET mock, I got wrong: [list topics]. For each wrong topic: - Is it a NCERT knowledge gap? - Is it a conceptual misunderstanding? - Is it an assertion-reason format issue? Create a targeted revision plan." M-02 — Speed Analysis "I spent too much time on Biology in my mock. Typical time per NEET Biology question should be 45–60 seconds. What strategies help read-and-answer Biology MCQs faster? How do I identify the answer type quickly?" description: AI Atlas → Study Systems → NEET → Complete System Full AI-assisted study system for NEET UG. NCERT-first approach. 2-year and 1-year variants. --- NEET Struct
text: NEET — Complete AI Study System | AI Atlas Skip to content AI Atlas Menu Home Study Systems Prompt Library Workflows Tools Database Resources Career Guides About Search Contributing Roadmap GitHub Home / Study Systems / NEET / NEET — Complete AI Study System study-systems NEET — Complete AI Study System AI Atlas → Study Systems → NEET → Complete System Full AI-assisted study system for NEET UG. NCERT-first approach. 2-year and 1-year variants. NEET Structure Subject Questions Marks Strategy Biology 90 (50 Botany + 40 Zoology) 360 Primary focus Physics 45 180 Secondary focus Chemistry 45 180 Secondary focus Total 180 720 Rule #1: Biology gets 60% of your time. Always. NCERT-First Rule Every single word of NCERT Biology Class 11 and 12 must be memorised before you open any reference book. NEET Biology questions are almost entirely NCERT-based. Students who read H.C. Verma for Biology are wasting time. AI's role in NCERT mastery: "I'm reading NCERT Biology Class 11 Chapter [X — Chapter Name]. After I finish reading, I'll ask you questions. Right now, create a list of 20 things from this chapter that NEET has previously tested. I'll use it as my reading guide." Your AI Tool Stack Tool Purpose Cost Claude.ai Concept understanding, diagram description Free Gemini Hindi explanations, PDF upload Free Anki Biology terms, Physics formulas Free 2-Year Plan (Class 11 + 12) Class 11 Year Term 1 (June–September): Biology: Cell Biology (Chapters 8–10) + Biomolecules Physics: Laws of Motion + Work Energy + Thermodynamics Chemistry: Mole Concept + Atomic Structure + Chemical Bonding Term 2 (October–January): Biology: Plant Kingdom + Morphology + Anatomy + Ecology Physics: Gravitation + Properties of Matter Chemistry: Equilibrium + Redox + Hydrogen + s-Block Term 3 (February–May): Biology: Plant Physiology (Photosynthesis + Respiration + Growth) Physics: Oscillations + Waves + Kinetic Theory Chemistry: Organic Basics + Hydrocarbons + Environmental Class 12 Year Term 1 (June–September): Biology: Reproduction (Sexual + Asexual) + Genetics (Mendelian) Physics: Electrostatics + Current Electricity Chemistry: Electrochemistry + Chemical Kinetics Term 2 (October–January): Biology: Molecular Genetics + Evolution + Human Health Physics: Magnetic Effects + EMI + Optics Chemistry: d-Block + Coordination + Biomolecules + Polymers Term 3 (February–April — Revision Only): Full NCERT revision (Biology 3x, Physics 2x, Chemistry 2x) Daily mock tests Error log review Diagram Mastery System NEET tests diagrams heavily. Build your diagram library: "I'm studying [CHAPTER] for NEET. List all diagrams that have been asked in NEET PYQs from this chapter. For each diagram: what it shows, what labels are typically asked, what questions are asked about it." Then draw each diagram from memory. Redraw until perfect. text: School — AI Prompt Library | AI Atlas Skip to content AI Atlas Menu Home Study Systems Prompt Library Workflows Tools Database Resources Career Guides About Search Contributing Roadmap GitHub Home / Prompt Libraries / School / School — AI Prompt Library prompts School — AI Prompt Library AI Atlas → Prompts → School 25 AI prompts for Class 9–12 CBSE students. Simple language, board exam format. --- For Class 9–10 Students S-01 — Concept Explain Explore Systems Browse Resources School — AI Prompt Library AI Atlas → Prompts → School 25 AI prompts for Class 9–12 CBSE students. Simple language, board exam format. For Class 9–10 Students S-01 — Concept Explainer (Simple) "I'm a Class [9/10] student. Explain [TOPIC] simply. Use a real-life example I can relate to. Then tell me what questions are asked about this in board exams." S-02 — Board Answer Writer "The board exam question is: [QUESTION] ([X] marks) I'm in Class [10/12]. Write the model answer. Highlight keywords in bold. Show diagram placement if needed." S-03 — Formula Sheet "Create a formula sheet for [CHAPTER] for Class [9/10]. Only formulas needed for board exams. Include: formula, what each letter means, units." S-04 — Science Diagram Guide "List all diagrams CBSE has asked in the last 5 years for Class [10] [subject] from [chapter]. Tell me what labels to draw and how many marks each carries." S-05 — Difference Tables "Create a difference table between [CONCEPT A] and [CONCEPT B] for Class [10] board exams. Include at least 5 differences. Use the CBSE tabular format." S-06 — Numerical Practice "Give me 5 numerical problems from [CHAPTER] at CBSE Class [10] board level. Show the solution step by step. Include the formula first." S-07 — Long Answer Guide "The 5-mark board question on [TOPIC] asks me to explain it. Structure the perfect answer with: - Introduction (1 line) - Main content (with sub-points) - Diagram reference if needed - Conclusion (1 line)" S-08 — Chapter Summary "Summarise NCERT Chapter [name] for Class [9/10] in 10 bullet points. Focus on what's likely to be asked in the board exam. Simple language." S-09 — MCQ Practice "Create 10 MCQs from [CHAPTER] for Class [10] at CBSE board level. Mix concept-based and definition-based questions. Include answer key." S-10 — Common Mistakes "What are the 5 most common mistakes Class [10] students make when answering questions about [TOPIC] in CBSE board exams? How do I avoid them?" For Class 11–12 Students S-11 — Derivation Guide "This derivation is important for Class 12 [Subject] boards: [derivation name]. Break it down step by step. Number each step. Explain WHY each step is valid. What diagram is needed, if any?" S-12 — Important Questions List "List the 10 most important questions from [Chapter] for CBSE Class 12 [Subject] board exam. Include: question, expected marks, key points to include." S-13 — Practical Viva Preparation "I have my Class 12 [Subject] practical exam. For experiment: [experiment name] Give me 10 expected viva questions with answers." S-14 — Case Study Practice "CBSE now includes case-study questions. Create a case study question on [TOPIC] for Class [11/12]. Include: the passage, 4 sub-questions, answer key." S-15 — Chapter Revision (30 min) "Give me a 30-minute revision plan for [CHAPTER] for Class [12] board exam. What to read, what to practise, what to memorise. Time it so I finish in 30 minutes."
